description: Demo of battlefield hazards (Tactical Crucible #32). A skirmish in a fire-scorched waystation turns dangerous on round 2, when a toppled brazier sets the floor alight — a SPREADING fire that grows each round and burns anything standing in it (DEX save for half). The terrain becomes a weapon: keep clear of the flames, herd the bandits into them, or have the GM resolve a SHOVE/kick that sends a foe into the fire. Verify: round 2 a fire erupts, it spreads each round, and creatures caught in it take fire damage; shove a bandit in and watch them burn.
customIntroduction: Three bandits hold the waystation yard, where a rusted iron brazier still smolders atop a cracked plinth — the floor around it slick with old lamp-oil. One wrong knock and the whole yard could go up.
customContext:
DEMO — INTERACTIVE TERRAIN & HAZARDS (#32). A COMBAT scene showcasing a dynamic battlefield hazard (the engine resolves it — narrate what the Event Log shows).

HOW IT WORKS:
1. On combat round 2, the `brazier_topples` trigger fires `spawn_hazard`: a SPREADING fire erupts in the middle of the yard (around tile 14,12). Each round it deals 2d6 fire (DEX save DC 13 for half) to every creature standing in it, and it GROWS by a ring of tiles — racing across the oil-slicked floor.
2. The fire is a tinted zone on the map; it damages friend and foe alike, so positioning matters — keep the player clear and herd the bandits toward it.
3. INTERACT with it: when the player tries to SHOVE or kick a bandit into the flames, resolve it as an improvised/shove action (resolve_improvised_action or the shove rules) and on success move_entity the bandit onto a burning tile — it'll take fire damage on the next round tick. Likewise the player can topple more of the brazier for a fresh hazard if you want (spawn another via narration is fine, but the engine fire is the real one).

NARRATE the spreading danger — the oil catching, the line of fire creeping outward, a bandit screaming as he's shoved into it. Don't fake the damage; the engine burns whoever stands in the flames. Composes with morale (#34, the burned break) and enemy roles (#35).
objective: Beat the bandits — but mind the fire. On round 2 a brazier topples and the floor ignites, spreading each round. Stay clear of the flames, or drive the bandits into them.

then.message: DEMO (#32): on round 2 a toppled brazier sets the oil-slicked floor alight — a SPREADING fire that grows each round and burns anything in it (DEX save for half). Stay clear, or shove the bandits in.

then.text: The brazier topples — burning oil races across the floor!
